title: SAC Jets Bomb IDP Displacement Camp in Shwebo Township — Monk Killed, Two Women Injured
detail: Myanmar junta fighter jets conducted seven bombing runs against a displacement camp in Shwebo Township, Sagaing Region on April 7, killing a Buddhist monk who had been teaching displaced children at the camp school and severely injuring two women. The attack struck a site housing war refugees — civilians who had fled earlier SAC operations and airstrikes elsewhere in Sagaing Region. Shwebo Township is within the junta's designated 'security operations' zone across Sagaing; the targeting of a displacement shelter continued a documented SAC pattern of attacking IDP camps and civilian refuge sites. Over 3.6 million people have been internally displaced in Myanmar since the 2021 coup, the majority sheltering in informal camps and jungle settlements across Sagaing, Magway, and Kachin regions.

title: Sustained Combat Enters Day 6 in Minbu-Saku Area, Magway — School Bombed, 10+ SAC Troops Injured
detail: Fighting between resistance forces and SAC troops in the Minbu-Saku area of Magway Region continued into its sixth consecutive day on April 7, with resistance sources reporting 10+ junta troops injured across the engagement. SAC forces deployed airstrikes in support of their ground units, with a 500-pound bomb striking a school building and injuring at least one civilian; two additional civilians were also wounded by airstrikes during the day's fighting. A separate SAC airstrike in Pauk Township, Magway on April 7 conducted five bombing runs but caused no human casualties. The sustained engagement in Minbu-Saku reflects PDF efforts to expand operational control southward from Sagaing Region into central Magway.

title: Multiple Resistance Ambushes Kill Four SAC Troops Across Mon State — Ye and Mudon Townships
detail: Resistance forces conducted two separate combat operations in Mon State on April 7, killing a total of four SAC soldiers. In Ye Township (southern Mon State), a PDF ambush killed one junta officer and two soldiers with no reported resistance casualties. Separately in Mudon Township — where junta troops had killed a civilian truck worker in an indiscriminate-fire incident on April 4 — fighters from the Ramanya Mon Army (an ethnic Mon resistance group) attacked junta positions and killed one SAC soldier. Mon State has seen expanding resistance operations since 2022, with PDF units and the Ramanya Mon Army increasingly coordinating to contest SAC control across townships bordering Karen State and Thailand.
